# Introduction

We compare gaming behaviour between young people who are NEET (not in education, employment, or training; age ≤ 25) and a 1:1 propensity-score-matched sample of non-NEET players matched on age, gender, ethnicity, and country, using platform telemetry (Steam, Xbox, Nintendo) restricted to the three months before and after each participant's intake survey.

## Top games

The table below ranks the ten most-played games (by total playtime) separately for matched NEET and non-NEET players, alongside the number and percentage of each group who played the game, minutes per player, and genre metadata from IGDB.


::: {.cell layout-align="center"}
::: {.cell-output-display}

## Time of day

The figure below shows NEET players' playtime share relative to non-NEET players at each hour of the day. Positive values indicate hours where NEET players account for a larger share of play; negative values indicate hours where non-NEET players are relatively more active.


::: {.cell layout-align="center"}
::: {.cell-output-display}
![NEET minus non-NEET difference in share of total playtime per hour (percentage points, local time). Bars above zero indicate hours where NEET players are proportionally more active; bars below zero indicate the reverse.](index_files/figure-typst/fig-time-of-day-1.svg){#fig-time-of-day fig-align='center' width=80%}
:::
:::


## Distributions

The raincloud plots below compare the matched groups on six variables: objective playtime from telemetry, self-reported play, gaming disorder symptoms (GDT, wave 1), life satisfaction, and the perceived value of gaming for work and daily routines.


::: {.cell layout-align="center"}
::: {.cell-output-display}
![Raincloud plots comparing matched NEET and non-NEET players on objective playtime (telemetry), self-reported play, gaming disorder symptoms (GDT, wave 1), and life satisfaction (mean across waves). Dots show individual participants; boxes show IQR and median; densities show the distribution shape.](index_files/figure-typst/fig-distributions-1.svg){#fig-distributions fig-align='center' width=80%}
:::
:::


## Social play modes

The figure below compares the share of playtime spent in competitive multiplayer and cooperative games between matched NEET and non-NEET players. Games are tagged as competitive if IGDB lists Multiplayer, Massively Multiplayer Online, or Battle Royale modes, and as cooperative if Co-operative or Split screen modes are listed.


::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

:::



::: {.cell layout-align="center"}
::: {.cell-output-display}
![Share of playtime in competitive and cooperative game modes for matched NEET and non-NEET players. Panels are independent (a game can be both competitive and cooperative, so shares need not sum to 100%). Points show group means; horizontal lines show bootstrap 95% confidence intervals.](index_files/figure-typst/fig-social-modes-1.svg){#fig-social-modes fig-align='center' width=80%}
:::
:::
